ogTitle: Cathundral vs Cattle Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cathundral and Cattle Creek.
heading: Cathundral vs Cattle Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cathundral, New South Wales and Cattle Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cathundral has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Cattle Creek (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cathundral or Cattle Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cathundral has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Cattle Creek scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cathundral scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Cattle Creek scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cathundral or Cattle Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cathundral has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
